description: Figure format standard for the Kempner Institute Computing Handbook. Apply these settings when creating original figures so they share a consistent, on-brand look. Prefer original figures over copyrighted images.

brand_color_notes.navy: Primary color, from the Kempner logo. Use for titles, key labels, borders, and dark fills.
brand_color_notes.crimson: Harvard Crimson, the accent color. Use sparingly for highlights, emphasis, and a single focal element.
brand_color_notes.periwinkle: Light tint, matches the handbook caption background. Use for light fills and light text backgrounds.
brand_color_notes.background: Very light lavender canvas applied behind the whole figure.

sequential_palette_note: Navy to periwinkle ramp for multi-category figures such as bar charts and matrices. Use white text on the two darker steps and navy text on the two lighter steps.

export.naming: snake_case and descriptive, for example gpu_selection_map.png

page_usage.directive: MyST {figure} directive with a width (for example width: 70%) and a name
page_usage.caption: One or two sentences describing the figure. No em dashes, and no inline bold or italic in the body text.
page_usage.credit: For original handbook figures, no external credit is needed. If the concept is adapted from a source, add a short note such as (Adapted from [Source](url).)

principles: Prefer original, openly shareable figures over copyrighted images.
Use the brand colors above and reserve crimson for accents and a single focal element.
Apply the light lavender background (#F4F5FC) for a consistent branded canvas.
Use a clear, legible sans-serif font and navy (#14154C) for titles and key labels.
Keep figure text concise and put detail in the page prose rather than the figure.
